Gilgai Farm steals the show on Day 2 at Inglis Premier

The most desirable horses on day two of the Inglis Premier Yearling Sale were no easier to buy than on day one, and the bids came thick and fast for a regally bred Kermadec filly, a grey son of Extreme Choice, and a spectacular Too Darn Hot colt.

The ever-changing situation in Dubai has many racing participants stuck there with the airport closed, while the horses are still safe and doing trackwork in anticipation of the Dubai World Cup meeting at the end of March.

Last year's G1 TJ Smith Stakes winner Briasa was amongst the stars fine-tuning their preparations at Warwick Farm's trials on Monday morning, where promising juvenile Peyton also drew closer to debut and Stardom proved too quick for her rivals.

Romantic Warrior continued to blaze the trail to Triple Crown glory by winning the G1 Hong Kong Gold Cup, but it was Toronado's name up in lights thanks to Stormy Grove's victory that has him firmly on the path for the G1 Hong Kong Derby.
In today's 'The TTR Ten' - Inglis Premier Yearling Sales Edition - Trent Edmonds from Trent Edmonds Thoroughbreds answers 10 rapid-fire questions.

The opening session of the Inglis Premier Yearling Sale drew its customary strong crowd on Sunday, where there were emotional highs for Penfolds Thoroughbreds and the Widden Stud team amid a solid day’s trading with key metrics lifting.

A Zoustar filly offered by Two Bays Farm turned the most heads on the first day of the 2026 Inglis Premier Yearling Sale, but there was also a flurry of attention for offspring of Ghaiyyath, Toronado, and Extreme Choice.

It is two months into the yearling sales season, and we take a moment to pause and reflect on how the season has started for some of the current crop of first season sires. Who has been meeting expectations, and who is patiently waiting for results beyond the ring?
